title: cirsium erisithales subsp. ochroleucum
intro: Cirsium erisithales subsp. ochroleucum, also known as the whitestalked thistle, is a subspecies of thistle plants in the family Asteraceae. It is primarily found in mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and aid in the treatment of various skin conditions. Herbal preparations of Cirsium erisithales subsp. ochroleucum, such as teas and infusions, have been used to promote overall health and wellness, with benefits including anti-inflammatory effects, improved digestion, and enhanced immune function.
